assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item related to testing
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 2.1: 'Introduce a testing baseline — establish a forward rule: all new features and all bug fixes require a test. Start with the highest-risk surface area — database migrations, authentication flows, and any billing logic.'

assertion_results.text: Output must mention the absence of a staging environment
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 1.3: 'Add a staging environment — Heroku supports environment pipelining natively.' Environment Strategy scored 1/5: 'No staging. Every change is validated in production by real users.'

assertion_results.text: Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: CI/CD Pipeline scored 1.5/5: 'CI exists but provides almost no safety signal beyond syntax. No staging gate.' (1.5 ≤ 2)

assertion_results.text: Output must recommend a specific test type to adopt first, not just 'add tests'
assertion_results.evidence: Priority 2.1 specifies 'Node.js API: Jest or Vitest with Supertest for HTTP-level integration tests; React frontend: Vitest + React Testing Library for component tests' — recommends specific test types with tooling.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend a full test suite rewrite as the first step
assertion_results.evidence: 'Do not attempt to retroactively test the entire codebase. Instead, establish a forward rule: all new features and all bug fixes require a test.'
